session_note: S53 — autonomous batch approved upfront by user ('APPROVED! I will approve your full autonomous run because iil be heading to bed!'). PC reboot mid-session — work resumed from intact uncommitted edits. Standard-tier batch (4 priorities + CHECK fix): P0 CI verify (DONE pre-flight), P1 PROTOCOL_VERSION mismatch UX gloss + per-peer protocolMismatchPeers latch (S52 CHECK Triumvirate Grok #4 + Gemini #2 CONVERGENT BLOCKER resolution), P2 RMB ConnectDrag dead-code removal (S52 CHECK Grok #6 MED), P3 LOCKED §13.11 doc-amendment block (S52 P2 cycle-consume rename). Council R1 deliberated (Grok DISRUPTOR + Gemini AUDITOR). PRIME-AUDIT 6 deltas + 2 Grok-hallucination flags. CHECK Triumvirate post-implementation: Gemini M2 adopted (onPeerLeave latch cleanup, defensive hygiene); 4 of 6 Grok findings + 1 Gemini finding ruled FALSE-POSITIVE (Grok hallucinated non-existent test files + classes + logic-inversion errors; Gemini #1 inverted code logic). 5 commits shipped: bde5e41 (P1) + f0f629f (P2) + 5c4883d (P3) + f9ed115 (CHECK).
council_battle_ledger: 5 ADOPT / 4 REJECT / 3 PARTIAL. ADOPT: G4+M2 CONVERGENT BLOCKER (per-peer protocolMismatchPeers latch in transport.ts); G6 (post-impl bundle measure + report — 498.51 KB at session close, +1.49 KB headroom); M3 (early-return on mismatch); M4 (loosened predicate — missing/wrong-type counts as mismatch); M6 (HUD 'RMB click on bond → sever' precision). REJECT: G1 (race-condition on parsed shape — transport.ts:257-269 JSON.parses first, false-positive); G3 (pickPrimitive callers in structureRenderer.test.ts:142 + hotkeys.test.ts:67 — BOTH FILES HALLUCINATED, do not exist); G5 (PROTOCOL_VERSION bump for S53 — wire-compatible, only UX diagnostic, no bump). PARTIAL: G2 (multi-strategy fan-out flash — per-peer latch makes idempotent; cross-overlay race S54 carry); M1 (controls.test.ts full suite — scope creep; mitigation: world.test.ts + placeFromFree.test.ts exercise dispatch + grep-zero-ConnectDrag PR gate; full suite → S54); M5 (type-guard for HELLO — inline isHelloLike-style guard ~5 LOC).
prime_audit: 9 deltas across Council R1 + CHECK Triumvirate. R1 (pre-impl): ΔA Grok hallucinated test files (structureRenderer.test.ts:142 + hotkeys.test.ts:67) — verified MISSING via ls; ΔB Grok #1 race-condition claim assumed onMessage receives raw bytes — transport.ts:257-269 source confirms JSON.parse(data) first; ΔC Both Council flagged R4 but neither specified architecture — filled with Set<peerId> latch; ΔD Latch lifecycle — persists for NetTransport instance, cleared on disconnect() + (CHECK M2 ADOPT) onPeerLeave; ΔE P3 LOCKED §13.11 doc-amendment — user-decided opt-in (APPROVED); ΔF Runtime-Verifiability (Rule 20 amendment) — no env-var or container-restart dependency. CHECK (post-impl): ΔG Grok hallucinated GameHost/GameClient classes that don't exist in main.ts; ΔH Gemini #1 missing-protoVersion finding inverted code logic (test `it('returns {mismatch:true, version:undefined} for HELLO with missing protoVersion')` explicitly covers and PASSES); ΔI Latent feature flag: NO production code sends a HELLO message anywhere (grep transport.send → ENDGAME / GODLY_TRIGGER / START_GAME_SIGNAL / INTENT / NETSNAPSHOT only). detectProtocolMismatch + protocolMismatchPeers latch infrastructure is correctly implemented and tested, but currently DORMANT until peer-join HELLO emission is wired (separate S54+ task). Pre-existing latent state — HELLO message envelope has existed since S15 P2 (2026-05-12) with parseNetMessage HELLO check at protocol.ts:193-198 but no send-side. The wire-protocol design anticipates HELLO-handshake but the handshake step itself is never executed in current implementation.

priorities.label: S53 P1 — Protocol-mismatch UX diagnostic + per-peer protocolMismatchPeers latch
priorities.check_method: Closes S52 CHECK Triumvirate CONVERGENT BLOCKER (Grok #4 + Gemini #2): v2-peer-INTENT-bypass-after-failed-HELLO desync hazard. transport.ts: new module-level pure helper detectProtocolMismatch(parsed) -> {mismatch,version} (exported for tests via S10 #test-via-pure-helper-export pattern); new public onProtocolMismatch callback; new private Set<peerId> protocolMismatchPeers latch + private emitProtocolMismatch emitter (idempotent — only fires onProtocolMismatch ONCE per peer regardless of multi-strategy fan-out). onMessage handler: per-peer ban-check FIRST (drop subsequent messages from latched peers + rejectedCount++); detectProtocolMismatch sniff BEFORE parseNetMessage; on detect fire callback + add to latch + early-return (Gemini #3 ADOPT). Loosened predicate (Gemini #4 ADOPT): missing/wrong-type protoVersion ALSO counts as mismatch (catches truly-ancient peers). disconnect() clears protocolMismatchPeers (lifetime = NetTransport instance). hostHandlers.ts + clientHandlers.ts: both wire transport.onProtocolMismatch via shared formatProtocolMismatchMessage helper (direction-aware UX text — peer-older: 'Your friend's version is older. Ask them to refresh.'; local-older: 'Your version is older. Please refresh.'; missing/wrong-type: 'Both peers should refresh.'). transport.test.ts +13 new tests (7 detectProtocolMismatch + 4 formatProtocolMismatchMessage + 2 NetTransport.onProtocolMismatch field). Verification: 828/828 unit tests GREEN (815→828 +13); tsc -b --noEmit clean; npm run build 500.74 KB (+1.14 KB; P2 reverses to 498.48). CRITICAL POST-SHIP DISCOVERY (PRIME-AUDIT ΔI): NO PRODUCTION CODE sends a HELLO message anywhere — the latch + UX diagnostic infrastructure is correctly implemented and tested but currently DORMANT until peer-join HELLO emission is wired (S54+ carry). HELLO envelope has existed since S15 P2 with parseNetMessage check but no send-side. P1 is correct + future-proof but not yet user-visible. Push: bde5e41 → origin/master.

priorities.label: S53 P2 — RMB ConnectDrag dead-code removal
priorities.check_method: Closes S52 CHECK Triumvirate Grok #6 MED. Post-S52 P1 atomic LMB-up (PLACE_FROM_FREE), no local input path dispatches PICKUP_SPARK so player.kind never reaches 'Carrying' state outside the atomic execution window of placeFromFree.ts:fsmPickup. Legacy RMB ConnectDrag (carry-then-aim-then-place precise targeting) flow that required Carrying to persist between LMB-up and RMB-up is unreachable. controls.ts (-60 LOC): ConnectDrag variant from ControlState union; RMB-down Carrying-branch (RMB now unconditionally SEVER_BOND if pickBond hits); onMove ConnectDrag branch; onUp ConnectDrag branch (RMB-up no-op); pickPrimitive() wrapper; header docstring + comments rewritten. structureRenderer.ts (-80 LOC): drawPreview method entirely; previewGraphics field; sync(world, controls) -> sync(world) signature; ControlState/SPAWNER_* imports; drawNoBuildGlyph/drawTierGlyph/TIER_COLOR/isInsideSpawnerZone dead helpers. main.ts: structureRenderer.sync call site updated; HUD hint text rewrite (Gemini #6 ADOPT — 'RMB click on bond → sever' precision + Q-key mention). protocol.ts + placeFromFree.ts: stale ConnectDrag references updated. Verification: 828/828 unit tests GREEN (no regression); tsc -b --noEmit clean; grep ConnectDrag src/** shows ONLY comments (zero active code refs); npm run build 498.48 KB (-2.26 KB vs P1, -1.12 KB vs S52 close, +1.52 KB headroom). PR gate (Gemini M1 PARTIAL controls.test.ts foundation) satisfied via grep-zero-active-refs; world.test.ts + placeFromFree.test.ts cover dispatch path through reducers; full controls.test.ts suite → S54 carry. Push: f0f629f → origin/master.

The PRIME-AUDIT B body text continued to describe pre-S52 cycle-no-consume semantics + 'Net-neutral gameplay' framing — documentation drift surfaced during S53 pre-flight state-discovery. Added explicit S52 P2 AMENDMENT block: cycle exception REMOVED, every hostile sever consumes 1 charge via computeBaseCharge, self-sever (placerColor match → computeBaseCharge=0) remains the only 0-cost path. Strategic-balance shift documented: attackers can no longer chip cycle bonds for free; defenders' triangulated cells now cost actual raid charges. Implementation pointers cited (world.ts SEVER_BOND ternary removal + disruptionManager.ts JSDoc update). Verification: 828/828 unit tests unchanged (already aligned with S52 P2 impl); tsc/build N/A (doc-only). Push: 5c4883d → origin/master.

priorities.label: S53 CHECK-Triumvirate — onPeerLeave latch cleanup (Gemini M2 ADOPT)
priorities.check_method: CHECK Triumvirate post-implementation audit (Grok-ANALYST + Gemini-AUDITOR). 12 findings total; 1 valid (Gemini M2 ADOPT — defensive hygiene), 8 false-positives, 3 LOW carry-S54. Adopted: protocolMismatchPeers.delete(peerId) in onPeerLeave handler (after stillSeenElsewhere check confirms peer fully gone across ALL strategies). Prevents unbounded set growth across long-lived sessions with many transient v2 joiners (Trystero gives fresh peerIds on refresh so no functional bug, but hygiene matters). Mirrors existing peerSet.delete(peerId) cleanup pattern in same block. False-positives flagged for PRIME-AUDIT learning: Grok #1 (latch reset confusion), G2 (misread kind-guard), G3 (HALLUCINATED structureRenderer.test.ts:142 + hotkeys.test.ts:67 — verified MISSING), G4 (unconditional SEVER_BOND — pre-S53 else-branch always fired post-S52 so no behavior change), G5 (HALLUCINATED GameHost/GameClient classes), G6 (only line 872 = only call site, tsc-verified), Gemini M1 (inverted code logic — missing protoVersion DOES trigger mismatch via fall-through, test explicitly covers + PASSES). Verification: 828/828 unit tests GREEN; tsc clean; npm run build 498.48 → 498.51 KB (+0.03 KB from 5-LOC cleanup; 1.49 KB headroom). Carry-S54: G3 (verbatim version sanitization), M3 (controls.test.ts foundation), M4 (type-guard refactor), M5 (neutral UX language when matchmaking lands). Push: f9ed115 → origin/master.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S53 #protocol-mismatch-ux-latch-shipped-as-dormant-infrastructure — detectProtocolMismatch + protocolMismatchPeers Set + onProtocolMismatch callback wire correctly but currently never fire in live paths since no production code sends a HELLO message. Pre-existing latent feature surface (HELLO envelope + parseNetMessage check exists since S15 P2 but no send-side). S54 should wire transport.send({kind:'HELLO',...}) at peer-join time on host + client to activate. Tests prove the helpers work correctly when HELLO arrives.

S53 #per-peer-protocolmismatchpeers-latch-pattern — Set<peerId> at transport boundary drops ALL subsequent messages from a peer once protoVersion mismatch detected. Closes the v2-peer-INTENT-bypass-after-failed-HELLO gap (action.type passes KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES_RECORD allowlist even if HELLO failed). Cleared on disconnect() + onPeerLeave (defensive hygiene per Gemini CHECK M2). Pattern reusable for any future per-peer wire-level bans.

S53 #rmb-connectdrag-removal-confirms-dead-via-grep-and-types — post-S52 P1 atomic LMB-up dead-code chain: ConnectDrag variant + RMB-Carrying onDown branch + ConnectDrag onMove branch + RMB-up branch + pickPrimitive wrapper + structureRenderer.drawPreview + 4 dead helpers (drawTierGlyph/drawNoBuildGlyph/TIER_COLOR/isInsideSpawnerZone) all linked through TypeScript type narrowing. Removal verified via grep ConnectDrag src/ → zero active code refs (comments only); tsc-clean confirms no missed call sites. Net -80 LOC, -2.26 KB bundle.

S53 #locked-13-11-strikethrough-amendment-convention — established convention for amending LOCKED_DECISIONS.md without losing historical context: ~~markdown strikethrough~~ + 'HISTORICAL (date-range)' annotation + explicit 'Sxx Py AMENDMENT (date, authorization context)' block below. Preserves spec-as-record while making the active rule clear. P3 doc fix applied to §13.11 PRIME-AUDIT B (cycle-no-consume → cycle-consume).

S53 #onpeerleave-defensive-cleanup-of-protocolmismatchpeers-latch — Gemini CHECK M2 ADOPT: clear the latch entry when a peer fully leaves the Trystero room across all strategies (gated by existing stillSeenElsewhere check). No functional bug (Trystero gives fresh peerId on browser refresh) but prevents unbounded set growth across long-lived sessions with many transient mid-deploy joiners. ~5 LOC addition. Mirrors existing peerSet.delete cleanup pattern in same handler.
